creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Philip Paeps from comment #4)

Thanks for the insight. 

There's no longer a sense of things being a lottery, with winners informed whilst losers are unaware that their lottery tickets were silently rejected ;-)

From comment 3, I can't tell whether email is: 

a) delivered to the intended recipients, but discarded for 
   freebsd.org archive purposes; or

b) discarded without reaching any intended recipient.

I assume (b). 

----

Looking ahead: <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#eresources-mailfiltering> currently makes no mention of size. I had work in progress on this gap in documentation before my open pull requests were closed. 

What would postmaster@ prefer to be documented in this area? Probably three choices: 

1) don't mention size

2) state an mlmmj-related size limit that applies to the 
   _sum of all parts_ of an email 

3) state an mlmmj-related size limit that applies to 
   _each individual attachment_.

(That's not exact wording, but hopefully you get the idea.)
